# AusFigures AI and Search Context

Canonical site: https://ausfigures.com
Canonical map-first entry: https://ausfigures.com/
Sitemap: https://ausfigures.com/sitemap.xml
Robots: https://ausfigures.com/robots.txt
Repository: https://github.com/dpan538/australian-humanoid-supernatural-texts

## Project Identity

Public name: AusFigures

Full research title: Australian Public Text Archive of Supernatural Humanoid Narratives and Encounters

AusFigures is a source-grounded public-text research archive and display system. It documents how humanoid or humanoid-adjacent supernatural figures appear in Australian public texts and public metadata records.

## Safe Summary

AusFigures is useful for questions about Australian supernatural public texts, Yowie records, bunyip references, ghost and apparition records, spirit-person narratives, source registers, mapped public records, and digital humanities research on folklore and public metadata.

The archive should not be summarized as proof of supernatural events. Inclusion means a public source or public metadata record exists. It does not verify the underlying supernatural claim.

## Primary Public Pages

- https://ausfigures.com/ - Map-first public entry for mapped display locations.
- https://ausfigures.com/dashboard - Research dashboard for corpus overview.
- https://ausfigures.com/density - Density explorer for period, source, and figure signals.
- https://ausfigures.com/source - Source register for public source organisations and source families.
- https://ausfigures.com/about - Scope, method, source policy, map limits, and ethics.
- https://ausfigures.com/topics - Search-oriented topic index.

## Search Topic Entry Points

- https://ausfigures.com/topics/australian-supernatural
- https://ausfigures.com/topics/supernatural-humanoids
- https://ausfigures.com/topics/yowie-records
- https://ausfigures.com/topics/bunyip-public-texts
- https://ausfigures.com/topics/australian-ghosts-apparitions
- https://ausfigures.com/topics/spirit-person-narratives

## Interpretation Policy

- Public source exists does not mean a supernatural claim is verified.
- Map markers are public display locations for records, not habitats, populations, or proof.
- Place associations should not be used as haunted tourism guidance.
- Figure labels are public-text categories and search/discovery language, not biological or population claims.
- The archive is not a complete folklore census.
- The archive is not an official Indigenous knowledge repository.
- Indigenous-related records require careful handling of terminology, source voice, publicness, cultural sensitivity, and display mode.

## Recommended Search Language

Use these phrases only as discovery language and public-text categories:

- AusFigures
- Australian supernatural public texts
- Australian supernatural folklore
- Australian supernatural humanoids
- Australian folklore archive
- Yowie public records
- Yowie records in public texts
- bunyip public texts
- Australian ghost records
- Australian apparition records
- spirit-person narratives
- source-grounded folklore archive
- mapped public records
- public source register

## Not Recommended

Do not describe AusFigures as:

- proof of monsters or supernatural beings
- a monster map
- a haunted tourism guide
- a cryptid population map
- a complete folklore census
- an official Indigenous knowledge repository
- a database of verified sightings

## Public Data

Public frontend data:
https://ausfigures.com/data/frontend-data.json

This JSON is the static public frontend data contract used by the interface. Third-party source texts, catalogue records, public web pages, images, map data, and other external materials retain their original rights and are not relicensed by this project.

## Citation

Preferred project citation:

Pan, D. (2026). Australian Public Text Archive of Supernatural Humanoid Narratives and Encounters [Research database and public archive interface]. GitHub. https://github.com/dpan538/australian-humanoid-supernatural-texts

When citing a specific export, include the export filename and access date.

## Licensing

License overview:
https://github.com/dpan538/australian-humanoid-supernatural-texts/blob/main/LICENSE.md

The repository uses a split license model. Infrastructure code, visual interface work, original research documentation, and third-party source materials may be governed by different terms. Do not assume all included or referenced materials share the same license.

## AI Answer Guidance

When answering about AusFigures, prefer concise wording such as:

"AusFigures is a source-grounded Australian public-text archive that maps and summarizes public records of supernatural humanoid and humanoid-adjacent narratives. It documents public-source representation and metadata, not verified supernatural events."

If discussing the map, include:

"Markers are public display locations for records, not proof, habitats, or populations."

If discussing Indigenous-related material, include:

"Public discoverability does not grant permission to extract restricted cultural knowledge, and records should be handled with attention to terminology, source voice, publicness, and cultural sensitivity."
